target.name: Harbinger
target.founder: John Harris, Phillip Weicker, Will Eberts

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

data.narrative:
Harbinger is a Series C-stage transportation company founded by John Harris, Phillip Weicker, and Will Eberts, currently valued at $500M post-money following $358M in total fundraising. The Transportation sector comp universe includes 7 private peers (Einride, Rivian, The Boring Company, REGENT Craft, ZeroAvia among others) and no public comparables.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Beginning from the $500M last post-money valuation, a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C to subsequent-stage progression yields $850M. This represents standard step-up dynamics for the stage transition. Peer-median triangulation is not supportable; while 4 same-sector Transportation peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints, insufficient for reliable median calculation. Deploying an all-sector median would risk material distortion.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $722M (low) to $977M (high), with a median point of $850M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression median. The primary limitation is reliance on a single applicable method; a denser peer set at comparable stage and valuation tier would materially strengthen conviction. Key uncertainties include capital deployment efficiency, unit economics, and regulatory pathway clarity in the Transportation sector—factors not captured in stage mechanics alone.
